pattern: Carry a flag down the recursion marking whether the current node is a left child, and add its value only when it is both a left child and a leaf.
hints: A node contributes only if two things are true at once: it has no children, and it is the left child of its parent — so the parent has to tell the child which side it is on.
Recurse with an extra piece of information saying 'you are a left child' or 'you are a right child'; the value at the bottom decides whether to count it.
At each node, recurse left flagged as left and right flagged as right; when you reach a leaf that was flagged left, add its value, otherwise add nothing.

edge_inputs.description: two left leaves at different depths
single node is a root, not a left leaf
root's left child is not a leaf so it does not count, but its left leaf does
a node that is a left child but has a right child is not a leaf
right-only chain has no left leaves

stress.description: random tree with holes, full value range
stress.note: first element non-null so a root exists
